texas_v10_rule_basis: Preserve noindex until all critical families are verified
District-grade routing must use direct local or district-owned evidence, not generic or statewide fallback
Legacy/inventory-only evidence does not satisfy California-grade completeness

final_blockers.evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 one more bounded live Idaho district pass on the exact residual Camas and Clark hosts. Camas root and `Contact Information` are still public HTTP 200 pages, but `https://www.camascountyschools.org/sitemap.xml` now returns HTTP 404. A fresh same-host href scan across the reviewed Camas pages bottoms out at `home`, `contact-information`, and `all-resources`; the rendered `all-resources` page now exposes same-host `Food Services`, `Federal Programs`, and `Advanced Opportunities` leaves, but those leaves still stay the wrong role. The live `Federal Programs` page only preserves Title I and Title IX compliance/training material plus coordinator contact, and the live `Advanced Opportunities` page only preserves accelerated-course guidance. Neither page exposes district-owned special-education, student-services, 504, Child Find, or procedural-safeguards routing. The district-linked Google Doc still exports only a board-roster lane rather than special-education routing. Clark root and `Parent Resources` still return HTTP 200, `https://www.clarkcountyschools161.org/sitemap.xml` now returns HTTP 404, and the older short aliases `/contact-us`, `/title-ix`, and `/parent-notification-of-general-education-instruction` now return HTTP 404. Their current replacement exact leaves remain live instead: `/about-us/contact-us-ccsd`, `/administration/title-ix`, and `/about-us/parent-notification-of-general-education-instruction` all return HTTP 200 on the same district host. A fresh same-host anchor-text scan across the live Clark `Parent Resources` page still bottoms out in wrong-role or too-thin lanes: the live replacement `Title IX` and `Parent Notification of General Education Instruction` leaves, food-service pages, a literacy plan, and the same district-hosted `Idaho Child Find` English and Spanish flyer PDFs, but no district-owned special-education, special-services, student-services, 504, or procedural-safeguards leaf with local contact evidence. Idaho therefore remains blocked because the final district-owned surfaces are still public but remain the wrong role or too thin for local special-education routing.

Reviewed 2026-06-25 one more bounded live Idaho DHW office-contract pass on the exact official office root, one exact office leaf, the paginated office inventory, and the now-public sitemap. `https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/offices` remains a live `Find a Service Location` root, `https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/dhw/caldwell-office` remains a live exact office leaf, the live paginated office inventory at `/offices?page=0`, `/offices?page=1`, and `/offices?page=2` still returns HTTP 200, and `https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/sitemap.xml` now returns HTTP 200. But those reviewed public surfaces still preserve office inventory only, not county assignment: the paginated office pages expose office names such as `Grangeville Office - Camas Resource Center` yet still expose no `county served`, `counties served`, `serves`, or `service area` field at all, and the public sitemap only enumerates exact office leaves such as Coeur d’Alene, Caldwell, Payette, Boise Westgate, Mountain Home, Burley, Twin Falls, Blackfoot, Pocatello, Idaho Falls, Rexburg, Salmon, and Kellogg. The county-named URLs that do appear on the same host are still unrelated food-bank or pantry pages like `adams-county-mobile-pantry-council`, `camas-county-mobile-pantry-fairfield`, and `clark-county-mobile-pantry-dubois`. The live DHW stack still preserves no county-served fields, county-to-office table, service-area contract, or county assignment export for disability-routing offices. Idaho county-local routing therefore remains an explicit split between the existing safe exact-office replacements and the legacy counties that still lack a public county contract.
